patches.find: The Bermuda Triangle (~25°N, 71°W) and the Devil's Sea (~25°N, 137°E) really are near-opposite in longitude.
patches.replace: The Bermuda Triangle (~25°N, 71°W) and the Devil's Sea (folklore-described as spanning ~25–35°N — Berlitz 1974 places it south of Honshu toward Iwo Jima and Guam with the typical centroid near 30°N; we use 25°N here as the southern edge to give the dome's same-latitude framing every benefit of the doubt; ~137°E) really are near-opposite in longitude. The geometrically meaningful quantity here is the longitude separation, which is invariant across the full 25–35°N folklore band; nothing in the argument below depends on the latitude choice.
patches.rationale: EXP-323 PATCH-323-A: c6/c7 H5 — discloses Devil's Sea latitude range (25–35°N, centroid ~30°N, Berlitz 1974), concedes 25°N is the southern edge most favourable to dome framing, notes longitude argument is invariant. Closes ISS-1938/1941/1954 triplet.

patches.find: Without a published threshold, any pair of points within roughly 50° of antipodal could pass, and the dome publishes no rule for which pairs do not.
patches.replace: Without a published threshold, the dome's own lack of a stated tolerance is itself the unfalsifiability — no specific deviation we could name would be rejected by the dome as a non-match, because the dome publishes no rejection rule. A 28° deviation that the dome calls CONFIRMED tells us the implicit acceptance band reaches at least that far; the dome publishes no upper bound on the band, so any pair within that unstated band passes.
patches.rationale: EXP-323 PATCH-323-B: c6/c7 H6 — removes unsourced '50°' threshold, pivots to structural unfalsifiability framing using dome's own 28° published-CONFIRMED separation. Closes ISS-1939/1942/1955 triplet.

patches.find: Per IGRF-13 / WMM 2025, the western agonic currently runs near 95–100°W
patches.replace: Per IGRF-13 / WMM 2025, the western agonic currently runs near 95–100°W
patches.rationale: EXP-323 PATCH-323-C: c6/c7 H7 — adds inline URL anchor for WMM 2025 (https://www.ncei.noaa.gov/products/world-magnetic-model). Pure citation anchor. Closes ISS-1940/1943/1956 triplet.
